Package com.apexfintechsolutions.ascendsdk.models.components

Class OptionOrder

java.lang.Object
com.apexfintechsolutions.ascendsdk.models.components.OptionOrder
public class OptionOrder extends Object
OptionOrder

The message describing an option order

    Time of the option order creation

    This represents the absolute value of the notional amount (without fees) transacted across all of this legs.

    This represents the direction of the total filled notional value, which will be present if the `cumulative_notional_value` is also present.

    Only "USD" is supported.

    System generated name of the option order.

    optionOrderId()
    System generated unique id for the option order.
    org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<OptionOrderOrderDate>
    orderDate()
    The date on which the option order will go to the market: must be "today".
    Optional<OptionOrderOrderRejectedReason>
    orderRejectedReason()
    When an option order has the REJECTED status, this will be populated with a system code describing the rejection.

    The processing status of the option order
    Optional<OptionOrderOrderType>
    orderType()
    The execution type of this option order.
    Optional<OptionOrderPriceDirection>
    priceDirection()
    The direction of the price, which indicates whether the entire option order should result in a credit to the account placing the order, or a debit from the account placing the order.

    The quantity of the option order to be placed.

    Special Reporting Instructions to be applied to this option order.

    timeInForce()
    Must be the value "DAY".

  • Constructor Details

    • OptionOrder

      public OptionOrder(Optional<String> accountId, Optional<? extends OptionOrderBrokerCapacity> brokerCapacity, Optional<String> cancelReason, Optional<? extends OptionOrderCancelRejectedReason> cancelRejectedReason, Optional<String> clientOrderId, org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<OffsetDateTime> clientReceivedTime, org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<OffsetDateTime> createTime, org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends OptionOrderCumulativeNotionalValue> cumulativeNotionalValue, Optional<? extends CumulativeNotionalValueDirection> cumulativeNotionalValueDirection, Optional<String> currencyCode, Optional<? extends List<TradingFee>> fees, org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<OffsetDateTime> lastUpdateTime, Optional<? extends List<OptionOrderLeg>> legs, org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends OptionOrderLimitPrice> limitPrice, Optional<String> name, Optional<String> optionOrderId, org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends OptionOrderOrderDate> orderDate, Optional<? extends OptionOrderOrderRejectedReason> orderRejectedReason, Optional<? extends OptionOrderOrderStatus> orderStatus, Optional<? extends OptionOrderOrderType> orderType, Optional<? extends OptionOrderPriceDirection> priceDirection, org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends OptionOrderQuantity> quantity, Optional<? extends List<OptionOrderSpecialReportingInstructions>> specialReportingInstructions, Optional<? extends OptionOrderTimeInForce> timeInForce)

    • OptionOrder

      public OptionOrder()

  • Method Details

    • accountId

      public Optional<String> accountId()
      The identifier of the account transacting this option order

    • brokerCapacity

      public Optional<OptionOrderBrokerCapacity> brokerCapacity()
      The capacity in which the broker is acting for this option order.

    • cancelReason

      public Optional<String> cancelReason()
      Used to explain why an option order is canceled

    • cancelRejectedReason

      public Optional<OptionOrderCancelRejectedReason> cancelRejectedReason()
      Used to denote when a cancel request has been rejected.

    • clientOrderId

      public Optional<String> clientOrderId()
      User-supplied unique option order ID. Cannot be more than 40 characters long.

    • clientReceivedTime

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<OffsetDateTime> clientReceivedTime()
      Required for any client who is having Apex do CAT reporting on their behalf.

    • createTime

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<OffsetDateTime> createTime()
      Time of the option order creation

    • cumulativeNotionalValue

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<OptionOrderCumulativeNotionalValue> cumulativeNotionalValue()
      This represents the absolute value of the notional amount (without fees) transacted across all of this legs. If present, this will always a positive number, or zero. This field will be empty if there are no leg executions.

    • cumulativeNotionalValueDirection

      public Optional<CumulativeNotionalValueDirection> cumulativeNotionalValueDirection()
      This represents the direction of the total filled notional value, which will be present if the `cumulative_notional_value` is also present. If there are no executions, this value will be absent. When the summed notional value of all CREDIT legs exceeds that of the DEBIT legs a CREDIT will be reported here; otherwise a DEBIT will be reported.

    • currencyCode

      public Optional<String> currencyCode()
      Only "USD" is supported. Full list of currency codes is defined at: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217

    • fees

      public Optional<List<TradingFee>> fees()
      Fees that will be applied to this option order.

    • lastUpdateTime

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<OffsetDateTime> lastUpdateTime()
      Time of the last option order update

    • legs

      public Optional<List<OptionOrderLeg>> legs()
      The legs of this option order. Only a single leg is allowed to be specified, and it must be for an OPTION security.

    • limitPrice

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<OptionOrderLimitPrice> limitPrice()
      The limit price for this option order. For single-leg option orders, this is the price per contract which the filled order must match or beat. For multi-leg orders, this represents the amortized "price per unit" that must be matched or beat. (E.g. with a limit price of a $4 DEBIT, and an order with 2 legs that includes one leg buy 100 shares of SBX and another leg to sell 1 contract of SBX, then the fills will be guaranteed to result in no more than a $400--4 USD X 100 shares/contract--total debit against the ordering account.)

    • name

      public Optional<String> name()
      System generated name of the option order.

    • optionOrderId

      public Optional<String> optionOrderId()
      System generated unique id for the option order.

    • orderDate

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<OptionOrderOrderDate> orderDate()
      The date on which the option order will go to the market: must be "today". Option orders will only be accepted during normal trading hours.

    • orderRejectedReason

      public Optional<OptionOrderOrderRejectedReason> orderRejectedReason()
      When an option order has the REJECTED status, this will be populated with a system code describing the rejection.

    • orderStatus

      public Optional<OptionOrderOrderStatus> orderStatus()
      The processing status of the option order

    • orderType

      public Optional<OptionOrderOrderType> orderType()
      The execution type of this option order.

    • priceDirection

      public Optional<OptionOrderPriceDirection> priceDirection()
      The direction of the price, which indicates whether the entire option order should result in a credit to the account placing the order, or a debit from the account placing the order.

    • quantity

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<OptionOrderQuantity> quantity()
      The quantity of the option order to be placed. The `ratio_quantity` of each leg will be multiplied by this value to determine the total number of units being ordered within each leg. (E.g. if a quantity of 3 is specified, and a leg with a `ratio_quantity` of 2 is specified, then the total number of contracts being ordered by that leg will be 6.)

    • specialReportingInstructions

      public Optional<List<OptionOrderSpecialReportingInstructions>> specialReportingInstructions()
      Special Reporting Instructions to be applied to this option order. Can include multiple Instructions.

    • timeInForce

      public Optional<OptionOrderTimeInForce> timeInForce()
      Must be the value "DAY". Regulatory requirements dictate the system captures the intended time_in_force, which is why this a mandatory field.

    • builder

      public static OptionOrder.Builder builder()

    • withAccountId

      public OptionOrder withAccountId(String accountId)
      The identifier of the account transacting this option order

    • withAccountId

      public OptionOrder withAccountId(Optional<String> accountId)
      The identifier of the account transacting this option order

    • withBrokerCapacity

      public OptionOrder withBrokerCapacity(OptionOrderBrokerCapacity brokerCapacity)
      The capacity in which the broker is acting for this option order.

    • withBrokerCapacity

      public OptionOrder withBrokerCapacity(Optional<? extends OptionOrderBrokerCapacity> brokerCapacity)
      The capacity in which the broker is acting for this option order.

    • withCancelReason

      public OptionOrder withCancelReason(String cancelReason)
      Used to explain why an option order is canceled

    • withCancelReason

      public OptionOrder withCancelReason(Optional<String> cancelReason)
      Used to explain why an option order is canceled

    • withCancelRejectedReason

      public OptionOrder withCancelRejectedReason(OptionOrderCancelRejectedReason cancelRejectedReason)
      Used to denote when a cancel request has been rejected.

    • withCancelRejectedReason

      public OptionOrder withCancelRejectedReason(Optional<? extends OptionOrderCancelRejectedReason> cancelRejectedReason)
      Used to denote when a cancel request has been rejected.

    • withClientOrderId

      public OptionOrder withClientOrderId(String clientOrderId)
      User-supplied unique option order ID. Cannot be more than 40 characters long.

    • withClientOrderId

      public OptionOrder withClientOrderId(Optional<String> clientOrderId)
      User-supplied unique option order ID. Cannot be more than 40 characters long.

    • withClientReceivedTime

      public OptionOrder withClientReceivedTime(OffsetDateTime clientReceivedTime)
      Required for any client who is having Apex do CAT reporting on their behalf.

    • withClientReceivedTime

      public OptionOrder withClientReceivedTime(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<OffsetDateTime> clientReceivedTime)
      Required for any client who is having Apex do CAT reporting on their behalf.

    • withCreateTime

      public OptionOrder withCreateTime(OffsetDateTime createTime)
      Time of the option order creation

    • withCreateTime

      public OptionOrder withCreateTime(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<OffsetDateTime> createTime)
      Time of the option order creation

    • withCumulativeNotionalValue

      public OptionOrder withCumulativeNotionalValue(OptionOrderCumulativeNotionalValue cumulativeNotionalValue)
      This represents the absolute value of the notional amount (without fees) transacted across all of this legs. If present, this will always a positive number, or zero. This field will be empty if there are no leg executions.

    • withCumulativeNotionalValue

      public OptionOrder withCumulativeNotionalValue(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends OptionOrderCumulativeNotionalValue> cumulativeNotionalValue)
      This represents the absolute value of the notional amount (without fees) transacted across all of this legs. If present, this will always a positive number, or zero. This field will be empty if there are no leg executions.

    • withCumulativeNotionalValueDirection

      public OptionOrder withCumulativeNotionalValueDirection(CumulativeNotionalValueDirection cumulativeNotionalValueDirection)
      This represents the direction of the total filled notional value, which will be present if the `cumulative_notional_value` is also present. If there are no executions, this value will be absent. When the summed notional value of all CREDIT legs exceeds that of the DEBIT legs a CREDIT will be reported here; otherwise a DEBIT will be reported.

    • withCumulativeNotionalValueDirection

      public OptionOrder withCumulativeNotionalValueDirection(Optional<? extends CumulativeNotionalValueDirection> cumulativeNotionalValueDirection)
      This represents the direction of the total filled notional value, which will be present if the `cumulative_notional_value` is also present. If there are no executions, this value will be absent. When the summed notional value of all CREDIT legs exceeds that of the DEBIT legs a CREDIT will be reported here; otherwise a DEBIT will be reported.

    • withCurrencyCode

      public OptionOrder withCurrencyCode(String currencyCode)
      Only "USD" is supported. Full list of currency codes is defined at: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217

    • withCurrencyCode

      public OptionOrder withCurrencyCode(Optional<String> currencyCode)
      Only "USD" is supported. Full list of currency codes is defined at: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217

    • withFees

      public OptionOrder withFees(List<TradingFee> fees)
      Fees that will be applied to this option order.

      Fees that will be applied to this option order.

      public OptionOrder withLastUpdateTime(OffsetDateTime lastUpdateTime)
      Time of the last option order update

      Time of the last option order update

    • withLegs

      public OptionOrder withLegs(List<OptionOrderLeg> legs)
      The legs of this option order. Only a single leg is allowed to be specified, and it must be for an OPTION security.

    • withLegs

      public OptionOrder withLegs(Optional<? extends List<OptionOrderLeg>> legs)
      The legs of this option order. Only a single leg is allowed to be specified, and it must be for an OPTION security.

    • withLimitPrice

      public OptionOrder withLimitPrice(OptionOrderLimitPrice limitPrice)
      The limit price for this option order. For single-leg option orders, this is the price per contract which the filled order must match or beat. For multi-leg orders, this represents the amortized "price per unit" that must be matched or beat. (E.g. with a limit price of a $4 DEBIT, and an order with 2 legs that includes one leg buy 100 shares of SBX and another leg to sell 1 contract of SBX, then the fills will be guaranteed to result in no more than a $400--4 USD X 100 shares/contract--total debit against the ordering account.)

    • withLimitPrice

      public OptionOrder withLimitPrice(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends OptionOrderLimitPrice> limitPrice)
      The limit price for this option order. For single-leg option orders, this is the price per contract which the filled order must match or beat. For multi-leg orders, this represents the amortized "price per unit" that must be matched or beat. (E.g. with a limit price of a $4 DEBIT, and an order with 2 legs that includes one leg buy 100 shares of SBX and another leg to sell 1 contract of SBX, then the fills will be guaranteed to result in no more than a $400--4 USD X 100 shares/contract--total debit against the ordering account.)

    • withPriceDirection

      public OptionOrder withPriceDirection(OptionOrderPriceDirection priceDirection)
      The direction of the price, which indicates whether the entire option order should result in a credit to the account placing the order, or a debit from the account placing the order.

    • withPriceDirection

      public OptionOrder withPriceDirection(Optional<? extends OptionOrderPriceDirection> priceDirection)
      The direction of the price, which indicates whether the entire option order should result in a credit to the account placing the order, or a debit from the account placing the order.

    • withQuantity

      public OptionOrder withQuantity(OptionOrderQuantity quantity)
      The quantity of the option order to be placed. The `ratio_quantity` of each leg will be multiplied by this value to determine the total number of units being ordered within each leg. (E.g. if a quantity of 3 is specified, and a leg with a `ratio_quantity` of 2 is specified, then the total number of contracts being ordered by that leg will be 6.)

    • withQuantity

      public OptionOrder withQuantity(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends OptionOrderQuantity> quantity)
      The quantity of the option order to be placed. The `ratio_quantity` of each leg will be multiplied by this value to determine the total number of units being ordered within each leg. (E.g. if a quantity of 3 is specified, and a leg with a `ratio_quantity` of 2 is specified, then the total number of contracts being ordered by that leg will be 6.)

    • withSpecialReportingInstructions

      public OptionOrder withSpecialReportingInstructions(List<OptionOrderSpecialReportingInstructions> specialReportingInstructions)
      Special Reporting Instructions to be applied to this option order. Can include multiple Instructions.

    • withSpecialReportingInstructions

      public OptionOrder withSpecialReportingInstructions(Optional<? extends List<OptionOrderSpecialReportingInstructions>> specialReportingInstructions)
      Special Reporting Instructions to be applied to this option order. Can include multiple Instructions.

    • withTimeInForce

      public OptionOrder withTimeInForce(OptionOrderTimeInForce timeInForce)
      Must be the value "DAY". Regulatory requirements dictate the system captures the intended time_in_force, which is why this a mandatory field.

    • withTimeInForce

      public OptionOrder withTimeInForce(Optional<? extends OptionOrderTimeInForce> timeInForce)
      Must be the value "DAY". Regulatory requirements dictate the system captures the intended time_in_force, which is why this a mandatory field.

      Overrides:
      equals in class Object

      Overrides:

    • toString
